title: Skopeo not working when "-" is present in FQDN
body:
Hello,

There is an open issue with skopeo, when the URL has a "-" in it the upload of images will fail.

example:

https://this-is-my-address.myapphub.com/

The issue has been present for a while:

[AAP skopeo ticket](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/6960629)

the odd part is that when installing AAP, if the URL for the automation platform is empty then the DNS record is taken, and the record does have "-" in it ( e.g. https://my-machine-dns-record )

Thank you for the review.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

If you are a Red Hat customer, please use Red Hat official support channels, to allow prioritization and to get the support you pay for.

---

That knowledge base article does not demonstrate any problem about `-` in host names. The actual root case here is:

> Use a fully qualified domain name (FQDN) instead of shortname for automation hub in the inventory file.

and that’s what needs changing to make the invoked `skopeo` command work. (Without a host name, `docker.io` is implied by the `docker://` syntax of `skopeo`.)

It’s also true that `_` in host names is invalid and rejected / not processed. The article is incorrect about `-`; that character is accepted, as long as it is not at the start or end of the host name.
